symbols: church doorway — the threshold between the profane and the sacred, Christ as the door (JHN.10.9)
weathered stone facade — the endurance of the Church through time
description: A close-up view of an ornate stone church doorway. The portal features carved columns and an arched surround with decorative moldings. The heavy wooden door is partially visible in shadow within the arch. The stone facade shows signs of age and weathering, with patches of warm golden and gray tones. A small urn or finial sits atop the arch. A sliver of blue sky is visible at the upper right. The brushwork is thick and textured, capturing the roughness of the ancient stonework.

scripture_refs.reason: Christ declares 'I am the door' — the church entrance symbolically represents access to God through Christ
